name: SYSTEM CLOCK
description: "The system clock is the primary clock input used for all synchronous logic operations."

name: SYSTEM RESET
description: "The system reset signal is asynchronous and active-low, clearing all registers and deasserting the chip select when asserted."

name: PERIPHERAL SELECT
description: "The peripheral select signal is used to select the SDC as an APB slave."

name: TRANSFER ENABLE
description: "Transfer enable marks the second cycle of an APB transfer."

name: WRITE ENABLE
description: "Write enable is used to indicate write operation, generating an active-low write strobe when asserted."

name: REGISTER ADDRESS
description: "Register address selects the target register for APB transfers."

name: WRITE DATA BUS
description: "Write data bus carries 32-bit data from the master to SDC during writes."

name: READ DATA BUS
description: "Read data bus carries 32-bit data from SDC to the master during reads."

name: TRANSFER ACKNOWLEDGE
description: "Transfer acknowledge is asserted high by SDC to complete APB transactions."

name: SERIAL CLOCK
description: "Serial clock is generated by SDC, with frequency set by the baud rate divisor."

name: MASTER DATA OUTPUT
description: "Master data output is serial data to the external slave, MSB first, shifted on the falling clock edge."

name: MASTER DATA INPUT
description: "Master data input is serial data from the external slave, sampled on the rising clock edge."

name: CHIP SELECT
description: "Chip select is an active-low output driven low at the start of a frame to address the external slave."

name: INTERRUPT REQUEST
description: "Interrupt request is asserted when transmission complete or parity error is detected."

name: TRANSMISSION COMPLETE
description: "Transmission complete is pulsed for one clock cycle when the last bit of a frame has been shifted out."

name: TRANSMIT ACKNOWLEDGE
description: "Transmit acknowledge is set when the remote slave confirms receipt of the last transmitted byte."

name: FIFO EMPTY STATUS
description: "FIFO empty status is asserted when the transmit buffer has no pending data and is ready for a new frame."

name: DATA VALID
description: "Data valid is asserted for one cycle when a new received byte is available in the receive buffer."

name: PARITY ERROR FLAG
description: "Parity error flag is set and held when a parity mismatch is detected on the received byte."

name: RECEIVE BUFFER FULL
description: "Receive buffer full is an active-low flag, deasserted when the receive buffer cannot accept additional bytes."

name: FLOW CONTROL ENABLE
description: "Flow control enable activates CTS/RTS-style handshaking when set in the control register."

name: CONTROL REGISTER
description:
The CONTROL REGISTER is a crucial component that contains mode and enable bits for both the APB interface and the serial interface. According to the provided descriptions, the CONTROL REGISTER has a specific set of bits that control the mode and enable the APB interface and serial interface. The descriptions are nearly identical, with no contradictions, indicating a clear understanding of the CONTROL REGISTER's function. 

The CONTROL REGISTER's primary role is to manage the interactions between the APB interface and the serial interface, allowing for flexible configuration and control. By containing mode and enable bits, the CONTROL REGISTER enables the system to switch between different operating modes and to enable or disable the interfaces as needed. This suggests that the CONTROL REGISTER is a key component in the overall system architecture, providing a centralized mechanism for controlling the flow of data between different interfaces.

In summary, the CONTROL REGISTER is a vital component that provides control and configuration capabilities for the APB interface and serial interface. Its mode and enable bits allow for dynamic control of the system, enabling the system to adapt to different operating conditions and requirements. With its centralized control mechanism, the CONTROL REGISTER plays a critical role in ensuring the smooth operation of the system. Overall, the CONTROL REGISTER is an essential component that provides a flexible and configurable interface control mechanism.

name: BAUD RATE DIVISOR
description: "The baud rate divisor register sets the system clock divisor for SPI clock generation."

name: STATUS REGISTER
description: "The status register contains all status flags, including receive buffer full, FIFO empty status, transmit acknowledge, and more."

name: SIGNAL
description: "The SDC has several signals, including the system clock signal, system reset signal, and interrupt request signal."
